Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 278 upvotes, 25 comments
  2. LLM Architecture Gallery 53 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Human Organ Atlas 70 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. In Memoriam: John W. Addison, my PhD advisor 55 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Learning athletic humanoid tennis skills from imperfect human motion data 75 upvotes, 9 comments
  6. //go:fix inline and the source-level inliner 24 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. Animated 'Firefly' Reboot in Development from Nathan Fillion, 20th TV 32 upvotes, 3 comments
  8. Let your Coding Agent debug the browser session with Chrome DevTools MCP 92 upvotes, 16 comments
  9. Kniterate Notes 48 upvotes, 10 comments
  10. Show HN: GDSL – 800 line kernel: Lisp subset in 500, C subset in 1300 43 upvotes, 9 comments
  11. Show HN: Signet – Autonomous wildfire tracking from satellite and weather data 97 upvotes, 27 comments
  12. The 49MB Web Page 25 upvotes, 4 comments
  13. IBM, sonic delay lines, and the history of the 80×24 display (2019) 63 upvotes, 17 comments
  14. A most elegant TCP hole punching algorithm 197 upvotes, 78 comments
  15. Why Mathematica does not simplify sinh(arccosh(x)) 132 upvotes, 51 comments
  16. Generating All 32-Bit Primes (Part I) 66 upvotes, 22 comments
  17. Separating the Wayland compositor and window manager 137 upvotes, 57 comments
  18. Bus travel from Lima to Rio de Janeiro 41 upvotes, 15 comments
  19. C++26: The Oxford Variadic Comma 61 upvotes, 27 comments
  20. Autoresearch Hub 16 upvotes, 4 comments
  21. Glassworm Is Back: A New Wave of Invisible Unicode Attacks Hits Repositories 153 upvotes, 84 comments
  22. Show HN: What if your synthesizer was powered by APL (or a dumb K clone)? 58 upvotes, 27 comments
  23. Office.eu launches as Europe's sovereign office platform 143 upvotes, 94 comments
  24. Grandparents are glued to their phones, families are worried [video] 120 upvotes, 82 comments
  25. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 132 upvotes, 97 comments
  26. $96 3D-printed rocket that recalculates its mid-air trajectory using a $5 sensor 329 upvotes, 309 comments
  27. The 100 hour gap between a vibecoded prototype and a working product 196 upvotes, 258 comments
  28. Treasure hunter freed from jail after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold 158 upvotes, 216 comments
  29. Ask HN: How is AI-assisted coding going for you professionally? 66 upvotes, 81 comments
  30. Hollywood Enters Oscars Weekend in Existential Crisis 81 upvotes, 266 comments

Last generated 4:30am, 16 March 2026. Source here.
